Regarding this family history of our Savior, observe its main purpose. It is not a pointless family history. It is not one intended to boast, as those of important people often are. It proves that our Lord Jesus is from the nation and family from which the Messiah was expected to come. The promise of blessing was made to Abraham and his descendants; the promise of rule, to David and his descendants. It was promised to Abraham that Christ would descend from him; and to David that he would descend from him; therefore, unless Jesus is a son of David and a son of Abraham, he is not the Messiah. Now this is proven here through well-known records. When the Son of God chose to take our nature, he came close to us in our fallen, wretched condition; but he was completely free from sin. And while we read the names in his family history, we should not forget how low the Lord of glory stooped to save humankind.

Let us consider the circumstances under which the Son of God entered this world, so that we may learn to look down on the empty honors of this world when compared with piety and holiness. The mystery of Christ becoming a man is to be adored, not investigated curiously. It was arranged that Christ should share our nature, yet remain pure from the stain of original sin, which has been passed down to all people descended from Adam. Observe that God guides those who think deeply, not those who do not. God’s time to come with instruction for his people is when they are confused. Divine comfort most delights the soul when under the pressure of troubled thoughts. Joseph is told that Mary will bring forth the Savior of the world. He was to call his name Jesus, a Savior. Jesus is the same name as Joshua. And the reason for that name is clear: those whom Christ saves, he saves from their sins; from the guilt of sin by the merit of his death, and from the power of sin by the Spirit of his grace. In saving them from sin, he saves them from wrath and the curse, and all misery, here and in the future. Christ came to save his people, not in their sins, but from their sins; and so to redeem them from among humankind to himself, who is separate from sinners. Joseph did as the angel of the Lord had instructed him, quickly, without delay, and cheerfully, without questioning. By applying the general rules of the written word, we should in all aspects of our lives, especially the major changes, seek direction from God, and we will find this safe and comforting.
